notes: Runs with performance similar to on WIndows 10. Occasional drop in frame rate when moving into new areas. Mouse movement was restricted - switching to Windowed display and back to Fullscreen gave full mouse movement until changing windows. Crashed once in several hours of play.
title: Dishonored 2

notes:
Having disabled the window manager (xfwm)'s compositor, I get framerates fluctuating between 30-60 at 1920x1080, on mostly 'very high' & 'ultra' settings, with TXAA x2, and HBAO+ on.

Fluctuations in framerate don't cause any noticeable stuttering, and the game runs *almost* as well as it runs on a native Windows 10 installation.

With the compositor on, however, it's too stuttery to be playable. I had previously disabled Esync (required on Fedora for several other games); so I'm not sure if that's relevant.
title: Dishonored 2

notes: Played Dishonored 2 to completion (Low Chaos). The game runs okay but there is a lot of stuttering at the beginning of each mission. However if you use the async shader patched dxvk found here https://github.com/jomihaka/dxvk-poe-hack this will eliminate most stuttering. This can be installed by downloading the zip file under Releases and doing "protontricks 403640 --force setup_dxvk.verb" to install. In launch options use "DXVK_ASYNC=1 %command%". With my GTX 1080 with max settings, the frame rate can fluctuate from 55 to 120 fps.  Completed Dishonored 2 in about 27 hours.
title: Dishonored 2

notes: Amazing performance, runs all ultra @4k without any tweaking

notes:
After installing the dxvk-poe-hack from https://github.com/jomihaka/dxvk-poe-hack, the game runs smoothly with occasional light stutters. The framerate is a constant 60Hz at "High" quality settings.

notes: Game starts fine out of the box. It stutters a lot if you're playing the level the first time (probably vulkan shader caching issue). Stuttering was terrible on an SATA SSD but installing the game on a blazing fast NVME SSD (if available), lowering ingame quality, limiting framerate (to 60fps) and using Feral's gamemode did help alot. Couldn't tell any difference / performance gains while disabling Esync or D3D11 in proton though.
title: Dishonored 2

notes: Using Valve's new ACO mesa driver.. no stuttering!! Early report as I have only played the tutorial but I get a solid 60fps on high graphics, don't need to set any launch options either.

title: Dishonored 2

notes:
Previously, under mint 19 and nvidia 390 drivers the game was  unplayable. There was texture distortion(looked like static/glittering) and the problem of severe stuttering in areas I had not been in before.

Now, under mint 19.2 and the 430 nvidia drivers it runs nearly flawlessly.  The stuttering and texture problems are gone.  The only issue is every handful of game runs the game will go to a black screen when loading into the main game screen after the company logos, which is a nuisance running full screen. This is the reason I chose to give it a silver rather than platinum rating.

Otherwise I would say it runs flawlessly now and did not require any tweaks so others might have chosen a platinum rating.
title: Dishonored 2

notes: Worked perfect after i found out it was necessary to disable v-sync in game and force it via the Nvidia driver. Before this it was unplayable with v sync on even with lowest settings frames would drop as soon as you move.
